description: DB-backed integration: a closed ticket with a threaded internal-user reply inside cutoff reopens to the board's explicit reopen status and records the reply comment.
DB-backed integration: a closed ticket with a threaded internal-user reply inside cutoff falls back to the board default open status when no explicit reopen status is configured.
DB-backed integration: when board reopen-on-reply is disabled, a threaded reply on a closed ticket adds the comment without reopening.
DB-backed integration: a client reply beyond the board cutoff window creates a new ticket instead of attaching to and reopening the closed ticket.
DB-backed integration: a client reply with AI suppression disabled reopens the closed ticket and adds the reply comment.
DB-backed integration: with board AI suppression enabled and EE acknowledgement decider returning `ACK`, the client reply stays attached to the closed ticket without reopening.
DB-backed integration: with board AI suppression enabled and EE acknowledgement decider returning `NOT_ACK`, the client reply reopens the closed ticket.
DB-backed integration: when AI suppression is enabled but the tenant lacks `AI Assistant`, the client reply reopens normally without AI suppression.
DB-backed integration: invalid AI output or acknowledgement-decider failure falls back to normal reopen behavior for the client reply.
DB-backed integration: token-only inbound replies are still skipped before any reopen work occurs.
Unit/integration coverage: CE or non-EE runtime uses the default non-AI acknowledgement decider without importing EE AI modules.
DB-backed integration: decision metadata is persisted or logged in the chosen storage surface for reopen, cutoff, and AI suppression outcomes.
